GT LEARN #172 🪙 Factorial Trailing Zeroes EASY Math Counting Factors
n= exp: 2

📝 Problem Statement

Given an integer n, return the number of trailing zeroes in n! (n factorial). For example, 5! = 120 has 1 trailing zero, and 10! = 3628800 has 2 trailing zeros. Constraint: 0 ≤ n ≤ 10⁴. Must run in O(log n) time.

🧠 Solving Approach

Trailing zero banta hai jab ek 10 milta hai factorization mein. 10 = 2 × 5, aur n! mein 2's hamesha 5's se zyada hote hain. Toh humein bas 5 ke factors count karne hain. Multiples of 25 ek extra 5 dete hain, multiples of 125 ek aur extra, etc.
🎯 Core Formula: count = ⌊n/5⌋ + ⌊n/25⌋ + ⌊n/125⌋ + ... (Legendre's formula for prime 5)
♻️ Loop Pattern: Repeatedly do n = n // 5 and add to count, until n == 0.
🏁 Termination: Jab n 0 ho jaaye → while n > 0 false → exit + return count.
⏱ Time: O(log₅ n) 💾 Space: O(1)

🧪 Test Cases

T1small (no 5s yet)→ 0
n = 3
T2basic two iterations→ 2
n = 10
T325 gives extra factor→ 6
n = 25
T4three iterations→ 24
n = 100
T5edge: zero→ 0
n = 0
Output: —
0/0
←→R 100%
